# Inspectors reference

Inspectors describe the shape of a markdown corpus and return evidence — counts and distributions, never recommendations. They are the descriptive dual of [rules]({{< relref "../rules/_index.md" >}}) and drive the [`inspect`]({{< relref "commands.md" >}}) command. This page is generated from the inspector registry, so it always matches the shipped engine.

## Structural

Structural inspectors report corpus-level facts: how files parse and how their frontmatter is shaped.

- `walk_parse` — Count files and report how many parse and carry frontmatter.
- `frontmatter_shape` — Group files by their frontmatter key-set and report observed field types.

## Object

Object inspectors report the distribution of frontmatter fields — presence, types, values, ranges.

- `object_field_frequency` — Report, per frontmatter key, how many files contain it.
- `object_field_types` — Report, per key, the histogram of observed value types.
- `object_field_values` — Report, per key, value cardinality and a small value set when it looks like an enum.
- `object_field_numeric_range` — Report the observed min and max of numeric fields.
- `object_field_string_length` — Report the observed min and max length of string fields.

## Markdown

Markdown inspectors report body conventions: headings, sections, and code fences.

- `markdown_heading_shape` — Report single-H1, H1-matches-title, and heading-level-jump rates.
- `markdown_sections` — Report recurring section headings and how many files contain each.
- `markdown_code_fences` — Report how many fenced code blocks open and how many carry a language tag.

## Filesystem

Filesystem inspectors report filename and path conventions across the corpus.

- `filesystem_naming` — Report filename casing, spaces, extensions, and nesting depth.
